proposed.claim: Make session: artifacts resolvable: evidence cites descriptive slugs (session:2026-06-10-substrate-position-discussion) that dereference to nothing, while the transcripts they name exist on disk as uuid-keyed jsonl files under ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-project>/. Provide the mapping - a session registry, or slug metadata stamped at capture time - so session: evidence dereferences the way document: and code: artifacts do.

evidence.detail: Surfaced while answering whether the thread was persisted: the transcript exists (8967b1b0-...jsonl, 4.3MB) but nothing links the session: slugs cited in a484-a488's evidence to it. The slug in this very evidence entry is an instance of the gap.

reasoning: Substantive tie: a slug-to-transcript resolvability mechanism must conform to c030 source-identifier taxonomy (authorship vs artifact sources). The directive extends the taxonomy reach; it does not contradict it. User-adjudicated 2026-06-11.
